commit.message:
fix: prevent stale duration_ms propagation on track change

- Add _actual_duration_ms field populated from stream_details.duration
- Add _best_duration_ms() helper preferring actual over Ynison state
- Reset duration_ms to 0 in sent state when advancing/going to previous
- Use _best_duration_ms() in all playback callbacks (_on_play/pause/seek)
- _advance_queue returns bool: True=next found, False=exhausted
- Exhausted queue sets _stream_stop_event for clean stream stop
- Update _update_metadata to prefer actual duration
- Add 3 new tests: actual duration preference, exhausted with actual, best_duration

Fixes premature track stop caused by stale duration from previous track.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

## Summary
- Pin all GitHub Actions to commit SHAs across all workflow templates
- Update auto-review to check all PR statuses (CI + CodeQL) before
approving
- Migrate auto-review from `app-id`/`@v2` to `client-id`/`@v3`

- Remove `dependency-review-action` (requires GHAS, replaced by Code
Security)
- Move inline `${{ }}` refs to `env:` vars in `run:` blocks for security
- Align PR template and CONTRIBUTING.md with template repo

- [ ] Workflow templates render correctly in Actions > New workflow

- [x] Changes match the intent of the linked Jira issue
- [x] Large binary assets use Git LFS

commit.message:
docs: add ConvNEF pipeline to technical report

Add Section 3.11 describing the gradient-free convolutional pipeline
architecture: PCA-based filter learning, multi-scale parallel stages,
spatial pyramid pooling, feature standardization, and ensemble
training.  All components are fully gradient-free.

Add Section 5.10 with comprehensive CIFAR-10 results from 7 sweep
rounds (80+ configurations on a T4 GPU).  Key results:
- PCA conv features lift flat-pixel 50.1% to 63.2% (+13.1%)
- Feature standardization is the largest single lever (+4.2%)
- Multi-scale parallel stages add +2.5% over single-scale
- 10-member ensemble with 30k neurons reaches 78.25%
- K-means filters, hierarchical stages, cutout, rectangular
  patches, and GCN all identified as negative results

Update Abstract to mention the 78.25% ConvNEF result.
Update Discussion 6.1 with ConvNEF competitive positioning.
Update strategy table (6.2) with ConvNEF row.
Update CIFAR-10 limitation (6.3) to reflect 78.3% vs prior 58%.
Add finding 13 to Conclusions covering ConvNEF.
Add Coates et al. (2011) reference for k-means filter comparison.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
analyzer: infer io.Reader invoke as EOF

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace BearSSL amalgamation with git submodule

The amalgamation approach failed due to ~20+ static symbol collisions
(K, block_size, P256_P, point_add, ccert_vtable, etc.) that BearSSL
assumes are file-local in separate compilation units.

Now using BearSSL as a proper git submodule:
- bearssl/ submodule from https://www.bearssl.org/git/BearSSL
- Taskfile: build_bearssl_lib() compiles all .c files -> libbearssl.a
  per architecture (build/, build_arm/, build_aarch64/, build_riscv/)
- l_tls.h: #include bearssl/inc/bearssl.h (public headers only)
- ci.ps1: git submodule update --init before Linux/ARM builds
- Non-TLS binaries are completely unaffected (no BearSSL linking)

CI: 10/10 targets pass (Win, Lin gcc+clang, ARM32 gcc+clang, AArch64 gcc+clang)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add --validate / -v option for cnct config validation (#84)

## Summary

Adds a `--validate` / `-v` CLI option that validates a `cnct.json`
config file without executing it. Output is written to stdout as JSON,
making it easy to consume in scripts and CI/CD pipelines.

## Output format

```json
{
  "valid": false,
  "issues": [
    {
      "severity": "error",
      "actionType": "link",
      "label": "My dotfiles",
      "message": "Source path does not exist: /home/user/.bashrc"
    }
  ]
}
```

Exit code is `0` when the config is valid, `1` when there are errors.

## Validation checks

| Task | Check |
|---|---|
| `link` / `copy` | Each source file or directory must exist on disk |
| `linkExpand` | Source directory must exist on disk |
| `cloneGitRepository` | Each repository URL must be a valid absolute
URI |
| `shell` | Shell type must be known; command must be non-empty |
| `environmentVariable` | Variable name must not contain `=` |

## Design

- Single `Validate(string configDirectoryRoot)` method on
`ICnctActionSpec` (replaces the old `void Validate()` that threw
exceptions)
- `CnctActionSpecBase` provides `CreateValidationError(message)` and
`CreateValidationWarning(message)` helpers so spec overrides don't
repeat `this.ActionType` / `this.Label`
- `CnctConfig.Validate()` returns `ConfigValidationResult` with an error
if `Actions` is null or empty
- Normal execution also calls `Validate()` first — errors block
execution, warnings are ignored

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Clippy] perf: convert Regex instances to source-generated [GeneratedRegex] (#196)

* perf: convert Regex instances to source-generated [GeneratedRegex]

Replace compiled/static Regex fields with source-generated partial methods
in SmlCellFormatter, Cell, HtmlConverter (Assembler), and DocumentAssembler.

- SmlCellFormatter: UnderRegex + CurrencyRegex (class made partial)
- Cell: XmlInvalidSymbolsRegex (class made partial)
- HtmlConverter (Assembler): DetectEntityRegex (class made partial)
- DocumentAssembler: DataImageTypeRegex, DataImageDataRegex,
  TemplateDirectiveSinglelineRegex added alongside existing
  TemplateDirectiveRegex; inline Regex.Match/Replace calls updated

Source-generated regexes avoid per-call compilation overhead and improve
startup allocation by emitting optimised code at build time.

commit.message:
Fix workflow syntax errors and shell script issues

Fixed critical issues in GitHub Actions workflows:

Changes in .github/workflows/deploy.yaml:
- Fixed malformed YAML indentation in nested steps
- Removed improperly nested 'Build Helm set flags' step
- Fixed ECR login command by separating pipe operator
  (aws ecr-public get-login-password > /tmp/ecr-token.txt && cat /tmp/ecr-token.txt | docker login)
- Updated IAM role name: github-runner-role -> github-action-role
- Updated ECR registry URL to use correct public.ecr.aws URL
- Fixed deployment file paths (removed kind-cluster subdirectory reference)

Changes in .github/workflows/eks-cluster/deploy.yaml:
- Fixed ECR login command by separating pipe operator
  (aws ecr-public get-login-password > /tmp/ecr-token.txt && cat /tmp/ecr-token.txt | docker login)

Root cause of 'line 14: -: command not found' error:
- YAML had improperly indented nested step causing dash character to be
  interpreted as a shell command instead of YAML step definition
- Pipe operator in complex bash one-liner was causing escaping issues

The workflows now properly:
1. Build Docker images
2. Authenticate to public ECR without credential exposure
3. Push images to public.ecr.aws
4. Deploy to Kubernetes clusters

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
revert: remove OOM CPU fallback from solvers

Remove the try/except torch.OutOfMemoryError CPU fallback from all
four solve paths: tikhonov, normal_equations, solve_from_normal_equations,
and StreamingNEFClassifier.solve().

The fallback masked genuine memory pressure problems and distorted
timing measurements — a silent CPU solve can take orders of magnitude
longer than a GPU solve, making benchmark results misleading.  Users
should instead size their models to fit GPU memory, or explicitly
move tensors to CPU before solving.

Reverts the functional changes from commit f472481.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Repo Assist] perf: cache property name/metadata lookups in getPropertyValues (#373)

* perf: cache property name/metadata lookups in getPropertyValues

Previously getPropertyValues called GetProperties() and GetCustomAttributes()
via reflection on every invocation. These are O(n) per call even for the
same type. The existing propCache for formatObject demonstrated the same
pattern.

Add propNameCache: ConcurrentDictionary<Type, (string * PropertyInfo)[]>
that memoises the (serialized-name, PropertyInfo) array for each type on
first use. Subsequent calls to getPropertyValues for the same type skip
both GetProperties and per-property GetCustomAttributes entirely.

This benefits APIs that use form-encoded or multipart bodies
(toFormUrlEncodedContent / toMultipartFormDataContent) for clients that
make repeated calls with the same model types.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

* test: add unit tests for getPropertyValues (JsonPropertyName, option unwrapping, cache path)

commit.message:
Build typed contract layer and execution skeletons (PR Milestone 2)

Add domain models, MCP schemas, service interfaces, and signal engine on top of foundation work.

## New Components

### Domain Layer
- **Macro domain** (domain/macro/):
  - `MacroIndicatorType`, `MacroSourceType`, `DataFrequency` enums
  - `MacroFeature` and `MacroSnapshot` models with validation
- **Signal domain** (domain/signals/):
  - `SignalType`, `SignalStrength`, `TrendDirection` enums
  - `SignalRule`, `SignalDefinition`, `SignalOutput`, `SignalResult` models
  - `SignalEngine` with deterministic evaluation (placeholder rule logic)
  - `evaluate_rules()` and rule evaluation functions

### Service Layer (services/)
- `MacroServiceInterface` and `SignalServiceInterface` contracts
- `MacroService` skeleton with feature fetching
- `SignalService` skeleton with engine orchestration

### MCP Schemas (mcp/schemas/)
- Base `MCPRequest` and `MCPResponse` classes
- `GetMacroFeatures` and `GetMacroSnapshot` request/response schemas
- `RunSignalEngine` request/response schemas for signal execution

### Tests & Documentation
- 124 unit tests covering all models, services, and MCP schemas
  - Domain model validation tests
  - Service interface contract tests
  - MCP schema validation tests
  - Signal engine and rule evaluation tests
- `docs/architecture_overview.md` - System design and data flow
- `docs/contracts.md` - Contract definitions and boundaries

## Key Design Decisions

1. **StrEnum for all enums** - Uses Python 3.12 StrEnum for cleaner serialization
2. **Pydantic validation** - All models validated at creation time
3. **Deterministic signal engine** - Same inputs always produce same outputs
4. **Placeholder-first approach** - Real implementations stubbed, focusing on contracts
5. **Interface-based services** - Abstract contracts enable testing and alternative implementations

## Validation Results

✅ Install: 147 packages resolved
✅ Lint: All checks passed, 57 files formatted
✅ Typecheck: No issues found in 57 source files
✅ Tests: 125 tests pass, 98% code coverage

## Out of Scope (v0.1)
- FastAPI route implementation
- Real data source integration
- Database persistence
- Autonomous trading decisions
- Deployment & containerization

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add GPU architecture check with CPU fallback in WhisperX script

The _detect_device() function now checks the GPU compute capability against
PyTorch's supported architecture list before selecting CUDA. If the GPU is
unsupported (e.g. older Pascal cards on newer PyTorch builds), it gracefully
falls back to CPU instead of crashing with a CUDA kernel error.
